mission.title: Install and Configure KServe on Kubernetes
mission.description: KServe is a standardized distributed generative and predictive AI inference platform for scalable, multi-framework deployment on Kubernetes. KServe is installed via kubectl apply from official GitHub releases or via OCI Helm charts, and requires cert-manager and a networking layer (Gateway API or Istio) as prerequisites.

steps.title: Install prerequisites: cert-manager
steps.description:
KServe requires cert-manager v1.15.0+. Install it if not already present:

```bash
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/cert-manager/cert-manager/releases/download/v1.16.3/cert-manager.yaml
```

Wait for cert-manager pods to be ready:

```bash
kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app.kubernetes.io/instance=cert-manager -n cert-manager --timeout=120s
```

steps.title: Install prerequisites: networking layer
steps.description:
KServe requires a networking layer. Choose one of the following:

**Option A: Gateway API (recommended for new installations)**
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/gateway-api/releases/download/v1.2.1/standard-install.yaml
```

**Option B: Istio (for Knative Serverless mode)**
Install Istio v1.22+ following the official Istio documentation, then install Knative Serving as the serverless layer.

steps.title: Install KServe
steps.description:
Install KServe using kubectl apply from the official release. The --server-side flag is required because the InferenceService CRD is large:

```bash
kubectl apply --server-side -f https://github.com/kserve/kserve/releases/download/v0.17.0/kserve.yaml
```

Then install the built-in ClusterServingRuntimes:

```bash
kubectl apply --server-side -f https://github.com/kserve/kserve/releases/download/v0.17.0/kserve-cluster-resources.yaml
```

Alternatively, install via OCI Helm charts:

```bash
helm install kserve-crd oci://ghcr.io/kserve/charts/kserve-crd --version v0.17.0
helm install kserve oci://ghcr.io/kserve/charts/kserve --version v0.17.0
```

steps.title: Configure deployment mode (optional)
steps.description:
For Standard mode with Gateway API, patch the KServe config:

```bash
kubectl patch configmap/inferenceservice-config -n kserve \
  --type=strategic \
  -p '{"data": {"deploy": "{\"defaultDeploymentMode\": \"Standard\"}"}}'

kubectl patch configmap/inferenceservice-config -n kserve \
  --type=strategic \
  -p '{"data": {"ingress": "{\"enableGatewayApi\": true, \"kserveIngressGateway\": \"kserve/kserve-ingress-gateway\"}"}}'
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the KServe controller is running:

```bash
kubectl get pods -n kserve
kubectl get crd | grep serving.kserve.io
```

uninstall.title: Remove KServe resources
uninstall.description:
Delete the KServe cluster resources and core components:

```bash
kubectl delete -f https://github.com/kserve/kserve/releases/download/v0.17.0/kserve-cluster-resources.yaml
kubectl delete -f https://github.com/kserve/kserve/releases/download/v0.17.0/kserve.yaml
```

Or if installed via Helm:

```bash
helm uninstall kserve
helm uninstall kserve-crd
```

uninstall.title: Clean up namespace
uninstall.description:
Remove the kserve namespace if no longer needed:

```bash
kubectl delete namespace kserve
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade KServe
upgrade.description:
Apply the new version manifests. Replace the version in the URLs:

```bash
kubectl apply --server-side -f https://github.com/kserve/kserve/releases/download/v<NEW_VERSION>/kserve.yaml
kubectl apply --server-side -f https://github.com/kserve/kserve/releases/download/v<NEW_VERSION>/kserve-cluster-resources.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the status of the KServe pods:

```bash
kubectl get pods -n kserve
```

troubleshooting.title: CRD apply fails
troubleshooting.description:
If applying KServe manifests fails, ensure you are using --server-side flag. The InferenceService CRD is too large for client-side apply:

```bash
kubectl apply --server-side -f https://github.com/kserve/kserve/releases/download/v0.17.0/kserve.yaml
```

troubleshooting.title: KServe controller not starting
troubleshooting.description:
Check that cert-manager is running and certificates are issued:

```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager
kubectl get certificates -n kserve
kubectl logs -l control-plane=kserve-controller-manager -n kserve
```

troubleshooting.title: InferenceService stuck in Unknown state
troubleshooting.description:
Check that the networking layer is properly configured and the ingress/gateway is working:

```bash
kubectl describe inferenceservice <name> -n <namespace>
kubectl get gateway -A
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of KServe will have the kserve-controller-manager pod running in the kserve namespace, cert-manager operational, and InferenceService CRDs registered. You can then create InferenceService resources to serve ML models.

prerequisites.description: Requires a Kubernetes cluster (v1.28+), kubectl, cert-manager v1.15.0+, and a networking layer (Gateway API recommended, or Istio for Knative Serverless mode).
